profile.name: Jefferson Evans Manadi
profile.tagline: I'm endlessly curious — I love wandering into unfamiliar problems, figuring out how things work, and building something useful on the way out. From systems to interfaces, I like making things that feel effortless to use.

intro.sub: You've found the corner of the internet where Jordan Reyes keeps the things they've built. Come wander.

about.lead: I'm an engineer who treats every project like an expedition into something I don't fully understand yet.
paragraphs: Over the past five years I've roamed across the stack — building distributed backends, shipping developer tools, and crafting front-ends that feel quick and playful. I'm just as happy deep in infrastructure as I am sweating the details of an interface.

What drives me is curiosity. I'd rather take on the unfamiliar problem and learn my way through it than repeat something I've already solved. Outside of work I tinker with electronics, contribute to open source, and over-engineer my home setup for fun.

timeline.title: Senior Engineer
timeline.desc: Lead the platform team behind a data pipeline serving millions of daily requests.

timeline.title: Software Engineer
timeline.desc: Built the real-time collaboration engine and internal tooling from zero.

timeline.title: B.S. Computer Engineering
timeline.desc: Focus on embedded systems and distributed computing.

projects.title: Atlas Dashboard
projects.summary: Real-time analytics dashboard handling 50k concurrent connections with sub-100ms updates.
description: Atlas visualises high-volume event streams as they happen. The challenge was keeping the interface responsive while ingesting tens of thousands of events per second.
I built a WebSocket layer with smart batching and a virtualised rendering pipeline, so the UI holds 60fps under heavy load. A custom caching strategy cut redundant queries by ~70%.

projects.title: Quill Engine
projects.summary: A distributed task scheduler with exactly-once delivery guarantees, written in Go.
description: Quill is a fault-tolerant scheduler for workloads that absolutely cannot run twice. It coordinates work across a cluster and persists state durably.
I implemented leader-election, the dedup layer, and a back-pressure system so the cluster degrades gracefully under spikes.

projects.title: Pulse Monitor
projects.summary: Low-power IoT sensor node running on-device ML for anomaly detection.
description: Pulse runs a lightweight model directly on a microcontroller to flag anomalies without a constant network connection.
I designed the firmware and power scheme that pushed battery life past six months, plus a quantised model that fits in under 64KB.

projects.title: Drift CMS
projects.summary: A headless content platform with a block-based editor and instant preview.
description: Drift is a headless CMS focused on editor ergonomics, with content modelled as composable, fully-typed blocks.
I built the collaborative block editor with conflict-free merging and an instant cross-device preview system.

projects.title: Sift
projects.summary: Semantic search over millions of documents with a custom embedding pipeline.
description: Sift makes large corpora searchable by meaning, not keywords. The hard part was making retrieval fast and cheap at scale.
I built the embedding pipeline and an approximate nearest-neighbour index returning results in milliseconds across millions of vectors.

projects.title: Relay
projects.summary: Open-source CLI for reproducible local dev environments. 2k+ stars.
description: Relay spins up reproducible dev environments from one config file, so 'works on my machine' stops being a problem.
I created the dependency-resolution engine and the plugin system the community now builds on.

contact.lead: Got a project, a role, or a wild idea? Let's explore it →
contact.sub: I'm open to new opportunities and good collaborations. Email is the fastest way to reach me — I usually reply within a day.
